title: Research Reveals China Quietly Profits from US AI Boom Despite Tech Restrictions
detail: New research indicates that Chinese companies are finding ways to benefit from the US artificial intelligence boom despite Washington's technology restrictions and export controls. The study suggests that indirect channels and partnerships allow Chinese firms to participate in the AI supply chain, highlighting the complexity of enforcing tech decoupling policies in an interconnected global technology ecosystem.

media.caption: Analysis shows Chinese companies finding ways to benefit from US AI development despite trade restrictions

title: Analysis Questions if US Hormuz Blockade Strategy Aims to Pressure China
detail: Strategic analysis examines whether recent US naval activities and discussions around potential Strait of Hormuz blockade scenarios are designed to apply economic pressure on China through disruption of critical energy supply routes. The analysis comes amid ongoing US-China tech decoupling and broader geopolitical tensions over semiconductor access and trade routes.

media.caption: Analysis of US strategic positioning in the Strait of Hormuz and potential implications for China
